content: Dosing principles: weight-based dosing (mg/kg) most common; body surface area (BSA) for chemotherapy and narrow TI drugs. Maximum doses should not exceed adult doses. Age-specific considerations: neonates require extended dosing intervals (immature clearance); children may need higher mg/kg doses than adults (phenytoin 5mg/kg/day vs adult 300mg); adolescents can use adult dosing. Formulation challenges: liquid formulations needed for young children; crushing tablets may alter release characteristics; palatability affects adherence. Off-label use common (70% of pediatric prescriptions) due to limited pediatric trials. Best Pharmaceuticals for Children Act (BPCA) and Pediatric Research Equity Act (PREA) incentivize/require pediatric studies. Avoid certain drugs: tetracyclines <8 years (teeth discoloration), fluoroquinolones <18 years (cartilage toxicity concern), aspirin <19 years with viral illness (Reye syndrome).
